content.content: 1 # Triton Autotune and Persistent Tuned Configs 2 3 FFPA's Triton backend can autotune forward and backward launch parameters for large-head-dimension attention. The autotune result can be persisted as a device-specific JSON file and reused later when runtime autotune is disabled. 4 5 This is useful for production inference or training jobs where you want stable startup latency and do not want each process to pay the Triton autotune cost. 6 7 ## Overview 8 9 There are two ways to use Triton tuned configs: 10 11 1. Run with runtime autotune enabled for one process: 12 13 ```python 14 from ffpa_attn import TritonBackend, ffpa_attn_func 15 16 out = ffpa_attn_func( 17 q, 18 k, 19 v, 20 forward_backend=TritonBackend( 21 forward=True, 22 autotune=True, 23 autotune_mode="fast" 24 ), 25 backward_backend=TritonBackend( 26 backward=True, 27 autotune=True, 28 autotune_mode="fast" 29 ), 30 ) 31 ``` 32 33 Triton benchmarks candidate configs and caches the best config in the current process. This is convenient for experiments, but the chosen config is not stored in the FFPA repository. 34 35 2. Generate persistent tuned configs once, then use the default non-autotune path: 36 37 ```bash 38 python -m ffpa_attn.autotune --mode fast --directions both --dtypes bf16,fp16 --overwrite 39 ``` 40 41 The generated JSON is saved under src/ffpa_attn/triton/configs/{device_name}.json , for example src/ffpa_attn/triton/configs/NVIDIA_L20.json. 42 43 Later calls with TritonBackend(..., autotune=False) will automatically load the matching 44 device config when it exists. 45 46 ## Generate Persistent Configs 47 48 Run the autotune generator from the repository root or from an installed FFPA environment: 49 50 ```bash 51 python -m ffpa_attn.autotune --mode fast 52 ``` 53 54 By default, the command refuses to overwrite an existing device config. Use 55 `--overwrite` when you intentionally want to regenerate it: 56 57 ```bash 58 python -m ffpa_attn.autotune --mode fast --overwrite 59 ``` 60 61 The generator defaults to B=1 and H=32. You can change them when your 62 deployment shape uses a different batch size or query-head count: 63 64 ```bash 65 python -m ffpa_attn.autotune --mode fast --B 1 --H 32 --overwrite 66 ``` 67 68 By default, the generated task grid covers the baseline no-mask, no-dropout, 69 equal-head cases. Add --full-tasks to also tune canonical attn_mask, 70 dropout, GQA, and MQA variants modeled after `python -m ffpa_attn.bench`: 71 72 ```bash 73 python -m ffpa_attn.autotune \ 74 --mode fast \ 75 --directions both \ 76 --dtypes bf16,fp16 \ 77 --full-tasks \ 78 --overwrite 79 ``` 80 81 --full-tasks can increase autotune time substantially because each additional variant is benchmarked separately. It is intentionally disabled by default so existing generation jobs keep their current coverage and runtime. You can generate only forward configs, only backward configs, or both: 82 83 ```bash 84 python -m ffpa_attn.autotune --mode fast --directions forward --overwrite 85 python -m ffpa_attn.autotune --mode fast --directions backward --overwrite 86 python -m ffpa_attn.autotune --mode fast --directions both --dtypes bf16,fp16 --overwrite 87 ``` 88 89 both is the default. The default dtype set is bf16. For benchmarks such as python -m ffpa_attn.bench that run both fp16 and bf16, generate both dtype configs explicitly: 90 91 ```bash 92 python -m ffpa_attn.autotune --mode fast --directions both --dtypes bf16,fp16 --overwrite 93 ``` 94 95 On SM90+ devices, add --enable-fwd-tma or --enable-bwd-tma to additionally generate persistent configs for the descriptor/TMA forward or backward path when each task shape supports it. The baseline fwd_generic and bwd_generic configs are still generated as compatibility fallbacks. Add --enable-fwd-ws or --enable-bwd-ws with the matching TMA flag when you also want warp-specialized TMA candidates. The legacy --enable-tma and --enable-ws flags remain as aliases that enable both directions: 96 97 ```bash 98 python -m ffpa_attn.autotune \ 99 --mode fast \ 100 --directions forward \ 101 --dtypes bf16,fp16 \ 102 --enable-fwd-tma \ 103 --enable-fwd-ws \ 104 --overwrite 105 ``` 106 107 ### Output Location 108 109 The default output directory is the package config directory: 110 111 ```text 112 src/ffpa_attn/triton/configs/ 113 ``` 114 115 The file name is derived from torch.cuda.get_device_name() with non-file-name characters replaced by underscores. For example: 116 117 ```text 118 NVIDIA L20 -> NVIDIA_L20.json 119 NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 -> NVIDIA_GeForce_RTX_5090.json 120 ``` 121 122 For smoke tests or CI, write to a temporary directory so partial configs are 123 not mistaken for full device configs: 124 125 ```bash 126 python -m ffpa_attn.autotune \ 127 --mode fast \ 128 --directions both \ 129 --overwrite \ 130 --output-dir /tmp/ffpa-config-smoke 131 ``` 132 133 At runtime, FFPA can also load configs from a custom directory with FFPA_TUNED_CONFIG_DIR: 134 135 ```bash 136 FFPA_TUNED_CONFIG_DIR=/tmp/ffpa-config-smoke python your_script.py 137 ``` 138 139 ### Multi-GPU Parallel Autotune 140 141 When multiple GPUs are available, you can distribute autotune tasks across 142 them with --num-gpus. This uses [Ray](https://docs.ray.io/) actors to ensure each 143 GPU runs exactly one autotune task at a time, avoiding resource contention: 144 145 ```bash 146 CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=4,5,6,7 \ 147 python -m ffpa_attn.autotune \ 148 --mode max \ 149 --directions both \ 150 --dtypes bf16,fp16 \ 151 --full-tasks \ 152 --num-gpus 4 \ 153 --overwrite 154 ``` 155 156 --num-gpus must not exceed the number of GPUs exposed by 157 CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES. The generator validates this at startup and exits with 158 a clear message when fewer GPUs are available than requested. 159 160 The parallel path uses a pool of Ray actors (one per GPU) and a queue-based 161 scheduler: each worker starts with one task, and as soon as a worker finishes 162 its task, it immediately receives the next pending task. This keeps all GPUs 163 busy while respecting the one-task-per-GPU constraint. 164 165 Results from all workers are merged into the same device JSON. The output 166 format, structure, and file path are identical to the single-GPU path. No 167 intermediate files are written by the workers. 168 169 --num-gpus defaults to None, which keeps the existing single-GPU 170 serial tuning path. On machines where Ray is not installed, omitting 171 --num-gpus avoids any Ray dependency. The Ray runtime is only imported 172 when --num-gpus is explicitly set. 173 174 #### Remote Ray Cluster 175 176 You can also submit autotune tasks to a remote Ray cluster by setting 177 --ray-address: 178 179 ```bash 180 python -m ffpa_attn.autotune \ 181 --mode max --full-tasks \ 182 --num-gpus 8 \ 183 --ray-address ray://my-cluster-head:6379 \ 184 --overwrite 185 ``` 186 187 When --ray-address is used, --num-gpus requests that many GPUs 188 from the cluster. The local CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES setting is ignored. 189 190 #### Development Smoke Tests with Ray 191 192 During development, combine FFPA_AUTOTUNE_MAX_CONFIGS with 193 --num-gpus for fast smoke tests: 194 195 ```bash 196 CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=4,5,6,7 \ 197 FFPA_AUTOTUNE_MAX_CONFIGS=4 \ 198 python -m ffpa_attn.autotune \ 199 --mode fast \ 200 --num-gpus 4 \ 201 --overwrite \ 202 --output-dir /tmp/ffpa-config-smoke 203 ``` 204 205 ## Autotune Modes 206 207 The generator supports the same mode names as the runtime Triton autotune path: 208 209 | Mode | Purpose | 210 | --- | --- | 211 | fast | Smaller search space. Recommended as the default persistent config mode. | 212 | max | Larger search space. Slower to generate, but may find better configs. | 213 214 The runtime lookup requires the mode to match. A JSON generated with --mode fast is used when triton_autotune_mode="fast"; a JSON generated with --mode max is used when triton_autotune_mode="max". 215 216 ## Shape Coverage 217 218 The generator tunes the following head dimensions: 219 220 ```text 221 320, 512, 640, 768, 1024 # headdim 222 ``` 223 224 The sequence-length grid is: 225 226 ```text 227 1, 512, 1024, 2048, 4096, 8192, 16384 # seqlen 228 ``` 229 230 The 1 entry is used only for decode query length (Nq=1`). Decode tuning does not generate Nkv=1 cases because a single-token KV cache is not a meaningful decode-attention benchmark target. 231 232 The 16384 sequence length is generated only when the current GPU has at least 48 GiB of memory. Smaller-memory devices skip it. 233 234 Persistent config generation tunes every target sequence length in this grid with exact Triton autotune keys. It does not reuse the online runtime seqlen buckets while generating JSON, so an entry for 512, 1024, or 2048 means that shape was benchmarked independently. Runtime lookup still performs reuse when the workload shape is not an exact persisted entry. The generated matrix covers: 235 236 | Direction | Kernels | 237 | --- | --- | 238 | Forward | generic forward, split-KV/decode stage1 | 239 | Backward | delta preprocess, main backward, decode backward stage1 | 240 241 Forward tasks include self-attention, cross-attention, decode attention (Nq=1), causal, and non-causal variants. 242 243 Backward tasks include main backward shapes (Nq >= 512) and decode backward 244 shapes (Nq=1, Nkv>1), with causal and non-causal variants. 245 246 When --full-tasks is enabled, the generator adds square prefill variants for 247 each tuned sequence length: 248 249 | Case | Shape / variant | 250 | --- | --- | 251 | attn-mask | Compact additive key-position mask [1, 1, 1, Nkv]. Backward tunes the bias-gradient path. | 252 | dropout | dropout_p=0.1, using the Triton dropout path. | 253 | gqa | Hq=H, Hkv chosen with the same divisor rule as python -m ffpa_attn.bench. | 254 | mqa | Hq=H, Hkv=1. | 255 256 These are single-feature canonical variants, not a full Cartesian product. For 257 example, --full-tasks tunes gqa and dropout separately, but it does not 258 generate a combined GQA+dropout case. 259 260 ## Runtime Lookup Rules 261 262 When runtime autotune is disabled, FFPA tries to load the current device JSON. If no compatible entry is found, it falls back to the built-in default launch parameters. Forward lookup filters by: 263 264 | Field | Meaning | 265 | --- | --- | 266 | direction | Must be forward. | 267 | kernel | fwd_generic or decode_fwd_stage1. | 268 | autotune_mode | Must match triton_autotune_mode. | 269 | dtype | fp16 or bf16. | 270 | causal | Must match is_causal. | 271 | has_attn_bias | Whether attn_mask / additive bias is present. | 272 | has_dropout | Whether dropout is active. | 273 274 Backward lookup additionally filters by: 275 276 | Field | Meaning | 277 | --- | --- | 278 | kernel | bwd_preproc, bwd_generic, or decode_bwd_stage1. | 279 | preprocess_d_chunk | Applies to the delta preprocess kernel. | 280 | bias_grad | Whether attention-bias gradients are requested. | 281 | grad_v_storage_dtype | Optional internal dV storage override. | 282 | use_gemv | Decode backward single-query specialization. | 283 | has_dropout | Whether dropout replay is active. | 284 | has_attn_bias | Whether additive bias is active. | 285 286 Generated entries may include nheads_q and nheads_kv for logging and JSON metadata. Runtime lookup can prefer an exact recorded head layout when one is available, but it does not require the head layout to match. Batch size and head count commonly vary across workloads, so FFPA reuses the same launch config across compatible mask/dropout/causal/kernel variants instead of missing the persistent config because Hq or Hkv changed. 287 288 Configs generated before these variant fields existed are treated as no-mask, 289 no-dropout entries. They can still satisfy baseline requests, while has_attn_bias and has_dropout continue to prevent semantically different kernel variants from being mixed. 290 291 After variant filtering, FFPA chooses the nearest persisted head dimension. Ties 292 prefer the larger candidate. Examples: 293 294 | Runtime D | Persisted D | 295 | --- | --- | 296 | 384 | 320 | 297 | 448 | 512 | 298 | 900 | 1024 | 299 300 For sequence length, FFPA chooses the smallest persisted sequence length that is 301 greater than or equal to the runtime value. If the runtime value is larger than 302 all persisted values, FFPA uses the largest available persisted value. Examples: 303 304 | Runtime seqlen | Persisted seqlen | 305 | --- | --- | 306 | 3000 | 4096 | 307 | 32768 | 8192 or 16384, depending on what was generated | 308 309 ### Debug Persistent Lookup 310 311 Set FFPA_LOGGER_LEVEL=DEBUG when you want to verify that runtime calls are 312 using persistent tuned configs instead of falling back to the built-in defaults: 313 314 ```bash 315 FFPA_LOGGER_LEVEL=DEBUG \ 316 FFPA_TUNED_CONFIG_DIR=/tmp/ffpa-config-smoke \ 317 python -m ffpa_attn.bench --fwd-backend triton --bwd-backend triton 318 ``` 319 320 On repeated runtime lookup hits, FFPA logs the kernel name and sanitized launch config selected from the in-process persistent config cache. The message uses debug_once semantics, so the same cache-hit/config line is emitted once per process instead of repeating on every attention call. 321 322 ## Development Smoke Tests 323 324 Full autotune generation can take a long time. Use FFPA_AUTOTUNE_MAX_CONFIGS to cap the number of shapes during development: 325 326 ```bash 327 CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 \ 328 FFPA_AUTOTUNE_MAX_CONFIGS=4 \ 329 python -m ffpa_attn.autotune \ 330 --mode fast \ 331 --directions both \ 332 --overwrite \ 333 --output-dir /tmp/ffpa-config-smoke 334 ``` 335 336 Then run a small workload with that temporary directory: 337 338 ```bash 339 CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 \ 340 FFPA_TUNED_CONFIG_DIR=/tmp/ffpa-config-smoke \ 341 python your_script.py 342 ``` 343 344 To compare persistent tuned configs against the built-in fallback launch defaults without removing the JSON, force runtime lookup to bypass persisted entries: 345 346 ```bash 347 CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 \ 348 FFPA_TUNED_CONFIG_DIR=/tmp/ffpa-config-smoke \ 349 FFPA_SKIP_PERSISIT_TUNED_CONFIG=1 \ 350 python your_script.py 351 ``` 352 353 For repository tests, a focused check is: 354 355 ```bash 356 pytest tests/test_persistent_autotune_config.py tests/test_triton_autotune_mode.py -q 357 ``` 358 359 ## Production Workflow 360 361 A typical workflow is: 362 363 1. Select the deployment GPU type. 364 2. Generate a full config on that GPU: 365 366 ```bash 367 CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python -m ffpa_attn.autotune \ 368 --mode fast --directions both --dtypes bf16,fp16 --full-tasks --overwrite 369 ``` 370 371 3. Commit the generated JSON under src/ffpa_attn/triton/configs/. 372 4. Run normal workloads with runtime autotune disabled, which is the default: 373 374 ```python 375 out = ffpa_attn_func(q, k, v) 376 ``` 377 378 If the runtime shape is outside the generated grid, FFPA uses the nearest compatible persisted config. If the device JSON is missing, malformed, or does not contain a compatible entry, FFPA silently keeps the existing built-in launch defaults. 379 380 ## Current Scope and Limitations 381 382 The first persistent-config generator focuses on the common no-bias and no-dropout path. The JSON schema already records bias_grad, has_dropout, and grad_v_storage_dtype, so bias-gradient and dropout-specific configs can be added later without changing the runtime lookup design. 383 384 decode_dq_reduce and key-bias gradient reduction are not currently autotuned by FFPA, so they keep their fixed launch parameters. Persistent configs are device-specific. Do not reuse a JSON generated on one GPU class as a performance baseline for a different GPU class. 385

The autotune result can be persisted as a device-specific JSON file and reused later when runtime autotune is disabled. This is useful for production inference or training jobs where you want stable startup latency and do not want each process to pay the Triton autotune cost. ## Overview There are two ways to use Triton tuned configs: 1. Run with runtime autotune enabled for one process: ```python from ffpa_attn import TritonBackend, ffpa_attn_func out = ffpa_attn_func( q, k, v, forward_backend=TritonBackend( forward=True, autotune=True, autotune_mode="fast" ), backward_backend=TritonBackend( backward=True, autotune=True, autotune_mode="fast" ), ) ``` Triton benchmarks candidate configs and caches the best config in the current process. This is convenient for experiments, but the chosen config is not stored in the FFPA repository. 2. Generate persistent tuned configs once, then use the default non-autotune path: ```bash python -m ffpa_attn.autotune --mode fast --directions both --dtypes bf16,fp16 --overwrite ``` The generated JSON is saved under src/ffpa_attn/triton/configs/{device_name}.json , for example src/ffpa_attn/triton/configs/NVIDIA_L20.json. Later calls with TritonBackend(..., autotune=False) will automatically load the matching device config when it exists. ## Generate Persistent Configs Run the autotune generator from the repository root or from an installed FFPA environment: ```bash python -m ffpa_attn.autotune --mode fast ``` By default, the command refuses to overwrite an existing device config. Use `--overwrite` when you intentionally want to regenerate it: ```bash python -m ffpa_attn.autotune --mode fast --overwrite ``` The generator defaults to B=1 and H=32. You can change them when your deployment shape uses a different batch size or query-head count: ```bash python -m ffpa_attn.autotune --mode fast --B 1 --H 32 --overwrite ``` By default, the generated task grid covers the baseline no-mask, no-dropout, equal-head cases. Add --full-tasks to also tune canonical attn_mask, dropout, GQA, and MQA variants modeled after `python -m ffpa_attn.bench`: ```bash python -m ffpa_attn.autotune \ --mode fast \ --directions both \ --dtypes bf16,fp16 \ --full-tasks \ --overwrite ``` --full-tasks can increase autotune time substantially because each additional variant is benchmarked separately. It is intentionally disabled by default so existing generation jobs keep their current coverage and runtime. You can generate only forward configs, only backward configs, or both: ```bash python -m ffpa_attn.autotune --mode fast --directions forward --overwrite python -m ffpa_attn.autotune --mode fast --directions backward --overwrite python -m ffpa_attn.autotune --mode fast --directions both --dtypes bf16,fp16 --overwrite ``` both is the default. The default dtype set is bf16. For benchmarks such as python -m ffpa_attn.bench that run both fp16 and bf16, generate both dtype configs explicitly: ```bash python -m ffpa_attn.autotune --mode fast --directions both --dtypes bf16,fp16 --overwrite ``` On SM90+ devices, add --enable-fwd-tma or --enable-bwd-tma to additionally generate persistent configs for the descriptor/TMA forward or backward path when each task shape supports it. The baseline fwd_generic and bwd_generic configs are still generated as compatibility fallbacks. Add --enable-fwd-ws or --enable-bwd-ws with the matching TMA flag when you also want warp-specialized TMA candidates. The legacy --enable-tma and --enable-ws flags remain as aliases that enable both directions: ```bash python -m ffpa_attn.autotune \ --mode fast \ --directions forward \ --dtypes bf16,fp16 \ --enable-fwd-tma \ --enable-fwd-ws \ --overwrite ``` ### Output Location The default output directory is the package config directory: ```text src/ffpa_attn/triton/configs/ ``` The file name is derived from torch.cuda.get_device_name() with non-file-name characters replaced by underscores. For example: ```text NVIDIA L20 -> NVIDIA_L20.json NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 -> NVIDIA_GeForce_RTX_5090.json ``` For smoke tests or CI, write to a temporary directory so partial configs are not mistaken for full device configs: ```bash python -m ffpa_attn.autotune \ --mode fast \ --directions both \ --overwrite \ --output-dir /tmp/ffpa-config-smoke ``` At runtime, FFPA can also load configs from a custom directory with FFPA_TUNED_CONFIG_DIR: ```bash FFPA_TUNED_CONFIG_DIR=/tmp/ffpa-config-smoke python your_script.py ``` ### Multi-GPU Parallel Autotune When multiple GPUs are available, you can distribute autotune tasks across them with --num-gpus. This uses [Ray](https://docs.ray.io/) actors to ensure each GPU runs exactly one autotune task at a time, avoiding resource contention: ```bash CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=4,5,6,7 \ python -m ffpa_attn.autotune \ --mode max \ --directions both \ --dtypes bf16,fp16 \ --full-tasks \ --num-gpus 4 \ --overwrite ``` --num-gpus must not exceed the number of GPUs exposed by CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES. The generator validates this at startup and exits with a clear message when fewer GPUs are available than requested. The parallel path uses a pool of Ray actors (one per GPU) and a queue-based scheduler: each worker starts with one task, and as soon as a worker finishes its task, it immediately receives the next pending task. This keeps all GPUs busy while respecting the one-task-per-GPU constraint. Results from all workers are merged into the same device JSON. The output format, structure, and file path are identical to the single-GPU path. No intermediate files are written by the workers. --num-gpus defaults to None, which keeps the existing single-GPU serial tuning path. On machines where Ray is not installed, omitting --num-gpus avoids any Ray dependency. The Ray runtime is only imported when --num-gpus is explicitly set. #### Remote Ray Cluster You can also submit autotune tasks to a remote Ray cluster by setting --ray-address: ```bash python -m ffpa_attn.autotune \ --mode max --full-tasks \ --num-gpus 8 \ --ray-address ray://my-cluster-head:6379 \ --overwrite ``` When --ray-address is used, --num-gpus requests that many GPUs from the cluster. The local CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES setting is ignored. #### Development Smoke Tests with Ray During development, combine FFPA_AUTOTUNE_MAX_CONFIGS with --num-gpus for fast smoke tests: ```bash CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=4,5,6,7 \ FFPA_AUTOTUNE_MAX_CONFIGS=4 \ python -m ffpa_attn.autotune \ --mode fast \ --num-gpus 4 \ --overwrite \ --output-dir /tmp/ffpa-config-smoke ``` ## Autotune Modes The generator supports the same mode names as the runtime Triton autotune path: | Mode | Purpose | | --- | --- | | fast | Smaller search space. Recommended as the default persistent config mode. | | max | Larger search space. Slower to generate, but may find better configs. | The runtime lookup requires the mode to match. A JSON generated with --mode fast is used when triton_autotune_mode="fast"; a JSON generated with --mode max is used when triton_autotune_mode="max". ## Shape Coverage The generator tunes the following head dimensions: ```text 320, 512, 640, 768, 1024 # headdim ``` The sequence-length grid is: ```text 1, 512, 1024, 2048, 4096, 8192, 16384 # seqlen ``` The 1 entry is used only for decode query length (Nq=1`). Decode tuning does not generate Nkv=1 cases because a single-token KV cache is not a meaningful decode-attention benchmark target. The 16384 sequence length is generated only when the current GPU has at least 48 GiB of memory. Smaller-memory devices skip it. Persistent config generation tunes every target sequence length in this grid with exact Triton autotune keys. It does not reuse the online runtime seqlen buckets while generating JSON, so an entry for 512, 1024, or 2048 means that shape was benchmarked independently. Runtime lookup still performs reuse when the workload shape is not an exact persisted entry. The generated matrix covers: | Direction | Kernels | | --- | --- | | Forward | generic forward, split-KV/decode stage1 | | Backward | delta preprocess, main backward, decode backward stage1 | Forward tasks include self-attention, cross-attention, decode attention (Nq=1), causal, and non-causal variants. Backward tasks include main backward shapes (Nq >= 512) and decode backward shapes (Nq=1, Nkv>1), with causal and non-causal variants. When --full-tasks is enabled, the generator adds square prefill variants for each tuned sequence length: | Case | Shape / variant | | --- | --- | | attn-mask | Compact additive key-position mask [1, 1, 1, Nkv]. Backward tunes the bias-gradient path. | | dropout | dropout_p=0.1, using the Triton dropout path. | | gqa | Hq=H, Hkv chosen with the same divisor rule as python -m ffpa_attn.bench. | | mqa | Hq=H, Hkv=1. | These are single-feature canonical variants, not a full Cartesian product. For example, --full-tasks tunes gqa and dropout separately, but it does not generate a combined GQA+dropout case. ## Runtime Lookup Rules When runtime autotune is disabled, FFPA tries to load the current device JSON. If no compatible entry is found, it falls back to the built-in default launch parameters. Forward lookup filters by: | Field | Meaning | | --- | --- | | direction | Must be forward. | | kernel | fwd_generic or decode_fwd_stage1. | | autotune_mode | Must match triton_autotune_mode. | | dtype | fp16 or bf16. | | causal | Must match is_causal. | | has_attn_bias | Whether attn_mask / additive bias is present. | | has_dropout | Whether dropout is active. | Backward lookup additionally filters by: | Field | Meaning | | --- | --- | | kernel | bwd_preproc, bwd_generic, or decode_bwd_stage1. | | preprocess_d_chunk | Applies to the delta preprocess kernel. | | bias_grad | Whether attention-bias gradients are requested. | | grad_v_storage_dtype | Optional internal dV storage override. | | use_gemv | Decode backward single-query specialization. | | has_dropout | Whether dropout replay is active. | | has_attn_bias | Whether additive bias is active. | Generated entries may include nheads_q and nheads_kv for logging and JSON metadata. Runtime lookup can prefer an exact recorded head layout when one is available, but it does not require the head layout to match. Batch size and head count commonly vary across workloads, so FFPA reuses the same launch config across compatible mask/dropout/causal/kernel variants instead of missing the persistent config because Hq or Hkv changed. Configs generated before these variant fields existed are treated as no-mask, no-dropout entries. They can still satisfy baseline requests, while has_attn_bias and has_dropout continue to prevent semantically different kernel variants from being mixed. After variant filtering, FFPA chooses the nearest persisted head dimension. Ties prefer the larger candidate. Examples: | Runtime D | Persisted D | | --- | --- | | 384 | 320 | | 448 | 512 | | 900 | 1024 | For sequence length, FFPA chooses the smallest persisted sequence length that is greater than or equal to the runtime value. If the runtime value is larger than all persisted values, FFPA uses the largest available persisted value. Examples: | Runtime seqlen | Persisted seqlen | | --- | --- | | 3000 | 4096 | | 32768 | 8192 or 16384, depending on what was generated | ### Debug Persistent Lookup Set FFPA_LOGGER_LEVEL=DEBUG when you want to verify that runtime calls are using persistent tuned configs instead of falling back to the built-in defaults: ```bash FFPA_LOGGER_LEVEL=DEBUG \ FFPA_TUNED_CONFIG_DIR=/tmp/ffpa-config-smoke \ python -m ffpa_attn.bench --fwd-backend triton --bwd-backend triton ``` On repeated runtime lookup hits, FFPA logs the kernel name and sanitized launch config selected from the in-process persistent config cache. The message uses debug_once semantics, so the same cache-hit/config line is emitted once per process instead of repeating on every attention call. ## Development Smoke Tests Full autotune generation can take a long time. Use FFPA_AUTOTUNE_MAX_CONFIGS to cap the number of shapes during development: ```bash CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 \ FFPA_AUTOTUNE_MAX_CONFIGS=4 \ python -m ffpa_attn.autotune \ --mode fast \ --directions both \ --overwrite \ --output-dir /tmp/ffpa-config-smoke ``` Then run a small workload with that temporary directory: ```bash CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 \ FFPA_TUNED_CONFIG_DIR=/tmp/ffpa-config-smoke \ python your_script.py ``` To compare persistent tuned configs against the built-in fallback launch defaults without removing the JSON, force runtime lookup to bypass persisted entries: ```bash CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 \ FFPA_TUNED_CONFIG_DIR=/tmp/ffpa-config-smoke \ FFPA_SKIP_PERSISIT_TUNED_CONFIG=1 \ python your_script.py ``` For repository tests, a focused check is: ```bash pytest tests/test_persistent_autotune_config.py tests/test_triton_autotune_mode.py -q ``` ## Production Workflow A typical workflow is: 1. Select the deployment GPU type. 2. Generate a full config on that GPU: ```bash CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python -m ffpa_attn.autotune \ --mode fast --directions both --dtypes bf16,fp16 --full-tasks --overwrite ``` 3. Commit the generated JSON under src/ffpa_attn/triton/configs/. 4. Run normal workloads with runtime autotune disabled, which is the default: ```python out = ffpa_attn_func(q, k, v) ``` If the runtime shape is outside the generated grid, FFPA uses the nearest compatible persisted config. If the device JSON is missing, malformed, or does not contain a compatible entry, FFPA silently keeps the existing built-in launch defaults. ## Current Scope and Limitations The first persistent-config generator focuses on the common no-bias and no-dropout path. The JSON schema already records bias_grad, has_dropout, and grad_v_storage_dtype, so bias-gradient and dropout-specific configs can be added later without changing the runtime lookup design. decode_dq_reduce and key-bias gradient reduction are not currently autotuned by FFPA, so they keep their fixed launch parameters. Persistent configs are device-specific. Do not reuse a JSON generated on one GPU class as a performance baseline for a different GPU class.

53	  DEFAULT_HEADDIMS,
54	  DEFAULT_SEQLENS,
55	  SCHEMA_VERSION,
56	  config_from_triton_config,
57	  default_config_dir,

62	)
63	from .logger import init_logger

65	_FULL_TASK_DROPOUT_P = 0.1
66	_TUNE_SEED = 42
67	
68	logger = init_logger(__name__)
69	
70	
71	@dataclass(frozen=True)
72	class TuneTask:
73	  """One runtime shape to autotune.
74	
75	  :param direction: ``"forward"`` or ``"backward"``.
76	  :param dtype: Activation dtype.
77	  :param headdim: Head dimension.
78	  :param seqlen_q: Query sequence length.
79	  :param seqlen_k: KV sequence length.
80	  :param causal: Whether to apply causal masking.
81	  :param nheads_q: Query-head count.
82	  :param nheads_kv: Key/value-head count.
83	  :param has_attn_bias: Whether to tune with an additive attention bias.
84	  :param has_dropout: Whether to tune with dropout enabled.
85	  :param case_name: Human-readable case name used in logs and JSON metadata.
86	  """
87	
88	  direction: str
89	  dtype: torch.dtype
90	  headdim: int
91	  seqlen_q: int
92	  seqlen_k: int
93	  causal: bool
94	  nheads_q: int = 32
95	  nheads_kv: int = 32
96	  has_attn_bias: bool = False
97	  has_dropout: bool = False
98	  case_name: str = "common"
99	
100	
101	def _parse_dtypes(value: str) -> list[torch.dtype]:
102	  mapping = {"fp16": torch.float16, "bf16": torch.bfloat16}
103	  dtypes: list[torch.dtype] = []
104	  for item in value.split(","):
105	    key = item.strip().lower()
106	    if key not in mapping:
107	      raise argparse.ArgumentTypeError(
108	        f"Unsupported dtype {item!r}; choose from fp16,bf16"
109	      )
110	    if mapping[key] not in dtypes:
111	      dtypes.append(mapping[key])
112	  if not dtypes:
113	    raise argparse.ArgumentTypeError("At least one dtype is required")
114	  return dtypes
115	
116	
117	def _resolve_directional_cli_flags(
118	  args: argparse.Namespace
119	) -> argparse.Namespace:
120	  """Resolve legacy global TMA/WS CLI flags into directional flags."""
121	  if args.enable_tma:
122	    args.enable_fwd_tma = True
123	    args.enable_bwd_tma = True
124	  if args.enable_ws:
125	    args.enable_fwd_ws = True
126	    args.enable_bwd_ws = True
127	  return args
128	
129	
130	def _dtype_schema_name(dtype: torch.dtype) -> str:
131	  if dtype == torch.float16:
132	    return "fp16"
133	  if dtype == torch.bfloat16:
134	    return "bf16"
135	  raise ValueError(f"Unsupported dtype {dtype!r}")
136	
137	
138	def _available_seqlens() -> list[int]:
139	  total_memory = torch.cuda.get_device_properties(
140	    torch.cuda.current_device()
141	  ).total_memory
142	  if total_memory >= 48 * 1024**3:
143	    return list(DEFAULT_SEQLENS)
144	  return [value for value in DEFAULT_SEQLENS if value < 16384]
145	
146	
147	def _resolve_gqa_heads(num_heads: int) -> int:
148	  """Choose a KV-head count for the canonical GQA full-task case.
149	
150	  :param num_heads: Query-head count.
151	  :return: KV-head count that divides ``num_heads``.
152	  """
153	  if num_heads <= 1:
154	    return 1
155	  candidate = max(1, num_heads // 4)
156	  while candidate > 1 and num_heads % candidate != 0:
157	    candidate -= 1
158	  return candidate
159	
160	
161	def _iter_full_variant_tasks(
162	  direction: str,
163	  dtype: torch.dtype,
164	  headdim: int,
165	  prefill_seqlens: list[int],
166	  heads: int,
167	) -> list[TuneTask]:
168	  """Return canonical full-coverage variants for one dtype/head-dim pair.
169	
170	  :param direction: ``"forward"`` or ``"backward"``.
171	  :param dtype: Activation dtype.
172	  :param headdim: Head dimension.
173	  :param prefill_seqlens: Sequence lengths used for square prefill cases.
174	  :param heads: Query-head count.
175	  :return: Extra mask/dropout/GQA/MQA tuning tasks.
176	  """
177	  tasks: list[TuneTask] = []
178	  gqa_heads = _resolve_gqa_heads(heads)
179	  for seqlen in prefill_seqlens:
180	    tasks.extend([
181	      TuneTask(
182	        direction,
183	        dtype,
184	        headdim,
185	        seqlen,
186	        seqlen,
187	        False,
188	        heads,
189	        heads,
190	        has_attn_bias=True,
191	        case_name="attn-mask",
192	      ),
193	      TuneTask(
194	        direction,
195	        dtype,
196	        headdim,
197	        seqlen,
198	        seqlen,
199	        False,
200	        heads,
201	        heads,
202	        has_dropout=True,
203	        case_name="dropout",
204	      ),
205	    ])
206	    if 1 < gqa_heads < heads:
207	      tasks.append(
208	        TuneTask(
209	          direction,
210	          dtype,
211	          headdim,
212	          seqlen,
213	          seqlen,
214	          False,
215	          heads,
216	          gqa_heads,
217	          case_name="gqa",
218	        )
219	      )
220	    if heads > 1:
221	      tasks.append(
222	        TuneTask(
223	          direction,
224	          dtype,
225	          headdim,
226	          seqlen,
227	          seqlen,
228	          False,
229	          heads,
230	          1,
231	          case_name="mqa",
232	        )
233	      )
234	  return tasks
235	
236	
237	def _iter_forward_tasks(
238	  dtypes: list[torch.dtype],
239	  seqlens: list[int],
240	  heads: int = 32,
241	  full_tasks: bool = False,
242	) -> list[TuneTask]:
243	  tasks: list[TuneTask] = []
244	  prefill_seqlens = [value for value in seqlens if value >= 512]
245	  decode_kv_seqlens = [value for value in prefill_seqlens if value > 1]
246	  for dtype in dtypes:
247	    for headdim in DEFAULT_HEADDIMS:
248	      for causal in (False, True):
249	        for seqlen_q in prefill_seqlens:
250	          for seqlen_k in prefill_seqlens:
251	            if causal and seqlen_k < seqlen_q:
252	              continue
253	            tasks.append(
254	              TuneTask(
255	                "forward",
256	                dtype,
257	                headdim,
258	                seqlen_q,
259	                seqlen_k,
260	                causal,
261	                heads,
262	                heads,
263	              )
264	            )
265	        for seqlen_k in decode_kv_seqlens:
266	          tasks.append(
267	            TuneTask(
268	              "forward",
269	              dtype,
270	              headdim,
271	              1,
272	              seqlen_k,
273	              causal,
274	              heads,
275	              heads,
276	              case_name="decode-attn",
277	            )
278	          )
279	      if full_tasks:
280	        tasks.extend(
281	          _iter_full_variant_tasks(
282	            "forward",
283	            dtype,
284	            headdim,
285	            prefill_seqlens,
286	            heads,
287	          )
288	        )
289	  return tasks
290	
291	
292	def _iter_backward_tasks(
293	  dtypes: list[torch.dtype],
294	  seqlens: list[int],
295	  heads: int = 32,
296	  full_tasks: bool = False,
297	) -> list[TuneTask]:
298	  tasks: list[TuneTask] = []
299	  prefill_seqlens = [value for value in seqlens if value >= 512]
300	  decode_query_seqlens = [1]
301	  decode_kv_seqlens = [value for value in prefill_seqlens if value > 1]
302	  for dtype in dtypes:
303	    for headdim in DEFAULT_HEADDIMS:
304	      for causal in (False, True):
305	        for seqlen_q in prefill_seqlens:
306	          for seqlen_k in prefill_seqlens:
307	            if causal and seqlen_k < seqlen_q:
308	              continue
309	            tasks.append(
310	              TuneTask(
311	                "backward",
312	                dtype,
313	                headdim,
314	                seqlen_q,
315	                seqlen_k,
316	                causal,
317	                heads,
318	                heads,
319	              )
320	            )
321	        for seqlen_q in decode_query_seqlens:
322	          for seqlen_k in decode_kv_seqlens:
323	            tasks.append(
324	              TuneTask(
325	                "backward",
326	                dtype,
327	                headdim,
328	                seqlen_q,
329	                seqlen_k,
330	                causal,
331	                heads,
332	                heads,
333	                case_name="decode-attn",
334	              )
335	            )
336	      if full_tasks:
337	        tasks.extend(
338	          _iter_full_variant_tasks(
339	            "backward",
340	            dtype,
341	            headdim,
342	            prefill_seqlens,
343	            heads,
344	          )
345	        )
346	  return tasks
347	
348	
349	def _limit_tasks(tasks: list[TuneTask]) -> list[TuneTask]:
350	  limit = max_configs_from_env()
351	  if limit is None:
352	    return tasks
353	  return tasks[:limit]
354	
355	
356	def _make_tensors(
357	  task: TuneTask,
358	  batch: int,
359	) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
360	  shape_q = (batch, task.nheads_q, task.seqlen_q, task.headdim)
361	  shape_kv = (batch, task.nheads_kv, task.seqlen_k, task.headdim)
362	  q = torch.randn(
363	    shape_q,
364	    device="cuda",
365	    dtype=task.dtype,
366	    requires_grad=task.direction == "backward",
367	  )
368	  k = torch.randn(
369	    shape_kv,
370	    device="cuda",
371	    dtype=task.dtype,
372	    requires_grad=task.direction == "backward",
373	  )
374	  v = torch.randn(
375	    shape_kv,
376	    device="cuda",
377	    dtype=task.dtype,
378	    requires_grad=task.direction == "backward",
379	  )
380	  return q, k, v
381	
382	
383	def _make_attn_bias(task: TuneTask) -> torch.Tensor | None:
384	  """Build the compact additive attention bias for a tuning task.
385	
386	  :param task: Tuning task metadata.
387	  :return: Additive attention bias, or ``None`` for no-mask tasks.
388	  """
389	  if not task.has_attn_bias:
390	    return None
391	  torch.manual_seed(_TUNE_SEED + 1)
392	  if task.direction == "backward":
393	    return (
394	      torch.randn(
395	        1,
396	        1,
397	        1,
398	        task.seqlen_k,
399	        dtype=torch.float32,
400	        device="cuda",
401	      ) * 0.25
402	    ).requires_grad_(True)
403	  return torch.randn(
404	    1, 1, 1, task.seqlen_k, dtype=task.dtype, device="cuda"
405	  ) * 0.25
406	
407	
408	def _entry_base(
409	  task: TuneTask,
410	  mode: str,
411	  kernel: str,
412	  config: dict[str, Any],
413	  enable_tma: bool | None = None,
414	  enable_ws: bool | None = None,
415	) -> dict[str, Any]:
416	  entry = {
417	    "direction": task.direction,
418	    "kernel": kernel,
419	    "causal": task.causal,
420	    "dtype": _dtype_schema_name(task.dtype),
421	    "headdim": task.headdim,
422	    "seqlen_q": task.seqlen_q,
423	    "seqlen_k": task.seqlen_k,
424	    "seqlen_q_bucket": autotune_seqlen_key(task.seqlen_q, mode),
425	    "seqlen_k_bucket": autotune_seqlen_key(task.seqlen_k, mode),
426	    "nheads_q": task.nheads_q,
427	    "nheads_kv": task.nheads_kv,
428	    "has_attn_bias": task.has_attn_bias,
429	    "has_dropout": task.has_dropout,
430	    "case_name": task.case_name,
431	    "config": config,
432	  }
433	  if enable_tma is not None:
434	    entry["enable_tma"] = enable_tma
435	  if enable_ws is not None:
436	    entry["enable_ws"] = enable_ws
437	  return entry
438	
439	
440	def _record_entry(
441	  entries: dict[tuple[Any, ...], dict[str, Any]],
442	  entry: dict[str, Any],
443	) -> None:
444	  key = (
445	    entry["direction"],
446	    entry["kernel"],
447	    entry["causal"],
448	    entry["dtype"],
449	    entry["headdim"],
450	    entry["seqlen_q"],
451	    entry["seqlen_k"],
452	    entry.get("preprocess_d_chunk"),
453	    entry.get("bias_grad"),
454	    entry.get("grad_kv_storage_dtype"),
455	    entry.get("use_gemv"),
456	    entry.get("enable_tma"),
457	    entry.get("enable_ws"),
458	  )
459	  if entry["kernel"] != "bwd_preproc":
460	    key += (
461	      entry.get("nheads_q"),
462	      entry.get("nheads_kv"),
463	      entry.get("has_attn_bias"),
464	      entry.get("has_dropout"),
465	    )
466	  entries[key] = entry

469	def _format_config(config: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
470	  """Return a compact JSON-style config string for progress logs.
471	
472	  :param config: Persisted Triton launch config.
473	  :return: Human-readable config string.
474	  """
475	  return json.dumps(config, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"))
476	
477	
478	def _format_entry(entry: dict[str, Any], choices_count: int, batch: int) -> str:
479	  """Return one progress-log description for a tuned entry.
480	
481	  :param entry: Persisted config entry.
482	  :param choices_count: Number of autotune candidates considered by the wrapper.
483	  :param batch: Batch size used for tuning.
484	  :return: Human-readable entry description.
485	  """
486	  direction = "FWD" if entry["direction"] == "forward" else "BWD"
487	  nheads_q = int(entry.get("nheads_q", 0))
488	  nheads_kv = int(entry.get("nheads_kv", nheads_q))
489	  shape = (
490	    f"{direction}:{entry['kernel']}("
491	    f"case={entry.get('case_name', 'common')},{entry['dtype']},"
492	    f"B{batch},Hq/Hkv={nheads_q}/{nheads_kv},"
493	    f"Q{entry['seqlen_q']},K{entry['seqlen_k']},D{entry['headdim']},"
494	    f"C{int(bool(entry['causal']))},mask={int(bool(entry.get('has_attn_bias', False)))},"
495	    f"drop={int(bool(entry.get('has_dropout', False)))},gqa={int(nheads_q != nheads_kv)}"
496	  )
497	  return f"{shape}) best[{choices_count}]={_format_config(entry['config'])}"
498	
499	
500	def _tune_forward(
501	  task: TuneTask,
502	  batch: int,
503	  mode: str,
504	  entries: dict[tuple[Any, ...], dict[str, Any]],
505	  enable_tma: bool = False,
506	  enable_ws: bool = False,
507	) -> list[tuple[dict[str, Any], int]]:
508	  q, k, v = _make_tensors(task, batch)
509	  attn_bias = _make_attn_bias(task)
510	  dtype = _dtype_schema_name(task.dtype)
511	  num_splits = _get_decode_num_splits(
512	    task.seqlen_q,
513	    task.seqlen_k,
514	    task.headdim,
515	    batch,
516	    task.nheads_q,
517	    q.device,
518	  )
519	  use_sm90_tma = enable_tma and is_sm90_tma_forward_supported(
520	    q,
521	    k,
522	    v,
523	    torch.empty_like(q),
524	    num_splits=num_splits,
525	  )
526	  use_sm90_ws = use_sm90_tma and enable_ws
527	
528	  def run_forward_tune(run_enable_tma: bool, run_enable_ws: bool) -> None:
529	    if task.has_dropout:
530	      torch.manual_seed(_TUNE_SEED + 17)
531	    out = ffpa_attn_func(
532	      q,
533	      k,
534	      v,
535	      attn_mask=attn_bias,
536	      dropout_p=_FULL_TASK_DROPOUT_P if task.has_dropout else 0.0,
537	      is_causal=task.causal,
538	      enable_gqa=task.nheads_q != task.nheads_kv,
539	      forward_backend=TritonBackend(
540	        forward=True,
541	        autotune=True,
542	        autotune_mode=mode,
543	        enable_tma=run_enable_tma,
544	        enable_ws=run_enable_ws,
545	      ),
546	    )
547	    del out
548	
549	  tuned_entries: list[tuple[dict[str, Any], int]] = []
550	
551	  run_forward_tune(False, False)
552	  if num_splits == 1:
553	    wrapper = _get_fwd_autotune(task.headdim, mode, dtype)
554	    entry = _entry_base(
555	      task,
556	      mode,
557	      "fwd_generic",
558	      config_from_triton_config(wrapper.best_config),
559	      enable_tma=False,
560	      enable_ws=False,
561	    )
562	    choices_count = len(wrapper.configs)
563	  else:
564	    use_gemv = task.seqlen_q == 1
565	    wrapper = _get_decode_fwd_stage1_autotune(
566	      task.headdim,
567	      use_gemv,
568	      mode,
569	      dtype,
570	    )
571	    entry = _entry_base(
572	      task,
573	      mode,
574	      "decode_fwd_stage1",
575	      config_from_triton_config(wrapper.best_config),
576	      enable_tma=False,
577	      enable_ws=False,
578	    )
579	    entry["use_gemv"] = use_gemv
580	    choices_count = len(wrapper.configs)
581	  _record_entry(entries, entry)
582	  tuned_entries.append((entry, choices_count))
583	
584	  if use_sm90_tma:
585	    run_forward_tune(True, use_sm90_ws)
586	    wrapper = _get_fwd_sm90_autotune(
587	      task.headdim,
588	      mode,
589	      dtype,
590	      enable_ws=use_sm90_ws,
591	    )
592	    entry = _entry_base(
593	      task,
594	      mode,
595	      "fwd_sm90_generic",
596	      config_from_triton_config(wrapper.best_config),
597	      enable_tma=True,
598	      enable_ws=use_sm90_ws,
599	    )
600	    choices_count = len(wrapper.configs)
601	    _record_entry(entries, entry)
602	    tuned_entries.append((entry, choices_count))
603	
604	  return tuned_entries
605

607	def _tune_backward(
608	  task: TuneTask,
609	  batch: int,
610	  mode: str,
611	  entries: dict[tuple[Any, ...], dict[str, Any]],
612	  enable_tma: bool = False,
613	  enable_ws: bool = False,
614	  enable_split_launch: bool = False,
615	) -> list[tuple[dict[str, Any], int]]:
616	  q, k, v = _make_tensors(task, batch)
617	  attn_bias = _make_attn_bias(task)
618	  dtype = _dtype_schema_name(task.dtype)
619	  use_sm90_tma = enable_tma and is_sm90_tma_backward_supported(
620	    q,
621	    k,
622	    v,
623	    q,
624	    q,
625	    k,
626	    v,
627	    seqlen_q=task.seqlen_q,
628	  )
629	  use_sm90_ws = use_sm90_tma and enable_ws
630	
631	  def run_backward_tune(
632	    run_enable_tma: bool,
633	    run_enable_ws: bool,
634	    run_enable_split_launch: bool = False
635	  ) -> None:
636	    if task.has_dropout:
637	      torch.manual_seed(_TUNE_SEED + 17)
638	    out = ffpa_attn_func(
639	      q,
640	      k,
641	      v,
642	      attn_mask=attn_bias,
643	      dropout_p=_FULL_TASK_DROPOUT_P if task.has_dropout else 0.0,
644	      is_causal=task.causal,
645	      enable_gqa=task.nheads_q != task.nheads_kv,
646	      forward_backend=TritonBackend(
647	        forward=True,
648	        autotune=True,
649	        autotune_mode=mode,
650	      ),
651	      backward_backend=TritonBackend(
652	        backward=True,
653	        autotune=True,
654	        autotune_mode=mode,
655	        enable_tma=run_enable_tma,
656	        enable_ws=run_enable_ws,
657	        split_launch=run_enable_split_launch,
658	      ),
659	    )
660	    out.float().sum().backward()
661	
662	  run_backward_tune(False, False)
663	
664	  pre_wrapper = _get_pre_autotune(False, task.headdim, mode, dtype)
665	  pre_entry = _entry_base(
666	    task,
667	    mode,
668	    "bwd_preproc",
669	    config_from_triton_config(pre_wrapper.best_config),
670	  )
671	  pre_entry["config"].setdefault(
672	    "BLOCK_HEADDIM",
673	    max(64, triton.next_power_of_2(task.headdim)),
674	  )
675	  pre_entry.update({
676	    "preprocess_d_chunk": False,
677	    "has_attn_bias": False,
678	    "has_dropout": False,
679	    "nheads_kv": task.nheads_q,
680	    "case_name": "common",
681	  })
682	  _record_entry(entries, pre_entry)
683	  pre_choices_count = len(pre_wrapper.configs)
684	  tuned_entries = [(pre_entry, pre_choices_count)]
685	
686	  if task.seqlen_q < 8:
687	    use_gemv = task.seqlen_q == 1
688	    wrapper = _get_decode_bwd_stage1_autotune(
689	      task.headdim,
690	      use_gemv,
691	      mode,
692	      task.has_attn_bias,
693	    )
694	    entry = _entry_base(
695	      task,
696	      mode,
697	      "decode_bwd_stage1",
698	      config_from_triton_config(wrapper.best_config),
699	    )
700	    entry.update({
701	      "bias_grad": task.has_attn_bias,
702	      "grad_kv_storage_dtype": None,
703	      "use_gemv": use_gemv,
704	    })
705	    choices_count = len(wrapper.configs)
706	  else:
707	    wrapper = _get_bwd_autotune(task.headdim, mode, task.has_attn_bias)
708	    entry = _entry_base(
709	      task,
710	      mode,
711	      "bwd_generic",
712	      config_from_triton_config(wrapper.best_config),
713	      enable_tma=False,
714	      enable_ws=False,
715	    )
716	    entry.update({
717	      "bias_grad": task.has_attn_bias,
718	      "grad_kv_storage_dtype": None,
719	    })
720	    choices_count = len(wrapper.configs)
721	  _record_entry(entries, entry)
722	  tuned_entries.append((entry, choices_count))
723	
724	  if task.seqlen_q >= 8 and enable_split_launch:
725	    run_backward_tune(False, False, run_enable_split_launch=True)
726	    dkdv_wrapper = _get_bwd_dkdv_autotune(
727	      task.headdim, mode, task.has_attn_bias
728	    )
729	    dkdv_entry = _entry_base(
730	      task,
731	      mode,
732	      "bwd_generic_dkdv",
733	      config_from_triton_config(dkdv_wrapper.best_config),
734	      enable_tma=False,
735	      enable_ws=False,
736	    )
737	    dkdv_entry.update({
738	      "bias_grad": task.has_attn_bias,
739	      "grad_kv_storage_dtype": None,
740	    })
741	    _record_entry(entries, dkdv_entry)
742	    tuned_entries.append((dkdv_entry, len(dkdv_wrapper.configs)))
743	
744	    dq_wrapper = _get_bwd_dq_autotune(task.headdim, mode)
745	    dq_entry = _entry_base(
746	      task,
747	      mode,
748	      "bwd_generic_dq",
749	      config_from_triton_config(dq_wrapper.best_config),
750	      enable_tma=False,
751	      enable_ws=False,
752	    )
753	    dq_entry.update({
754	      "bias_grad": False,
755	      "grad_kv_storage_dtype": None,
756	    })
757	    _record_entry(entries, dq_entry)
758	    tuned_entries.append((dq_entry, len(dq_wrapper.configs)))
759	
760	  if task.seqlen_q >= 8 and use_sm90_tma:
761	    run_backward_tune(True, use_sm90_ws)
762	    wrapper = _get_bwd_sm90_autotune(
763	      task.headdim,
764	      mode,
765	      dtype,
766	      task.has_attn_bias,
767	      enable_ws=use_sm90_ws,
768	    )
769	    entry = _entry_base(
770	      task,
771	      mode,
772	      "bwd_sm90_generic",
773	      config_from_triton_config(wrapper.best_config),
774	      enable_tma=True,
775	      enable_ws=use_sm90_ws,
776	    )
777	    entry.update({
778	      "bias_grad": task.has_attn_bias,
779	      "grad_kv_storage_dtype": None,
780	    })
781	    choices_count = len(wrapper.configs)
782	    _record_entry(entries, entry)
783	    tuned_entries.append((entry, choices_count))
784	
785	    if enable_split_launch:
786	      run_backward_tune(True, use_sm90_ws, run_enable_split_launch=True)
787	      dkdv_wrapper = _get_bwd_sm90_dkdv_autotune(
788	        task.headdim,
789	        mode,
790	        dtype,
791	        task.has_attn_bias,
792	        enable_ws=use_sm90_ws,
793	      )
794	      dkdv_entry = _entry_base(
795	        task,
796	        mode,
797	        "bwd_sm90_dkdv",
798	        config_from_triton_config(dkdv_wrapper.best_config),
799	        enable_tma=True,
800	        enable_ws=use_sm90_ws,
801	      )
802	      dkdv_entry.update({
803	        "bias_grad": task.has_attn_bias,
804	        "grad_kv_storage_dtype": None,
805	      })
806	      _record_entry(entries, dkdv_entry)
807	      tuned_entries.append((dkdv_entry, len(dkdv_wrapper.configs)))
808	
809	      dq_wrapper = _get_bwd_sm90_dq_autotune(
810	        task.headdim,
811	        mode,
812	        dtype,
813	        enable_ws=use_sm90_ws,
814	      )
815	      dq_entry = _entry_base(
816	        task,
817	        mode,
818	        "bwd_sm90_dq",
819	        config_from_triton_config(dq_wrapper.best_config),
820	        enable_tma=True,
821	        enable_ws=use_sm90_ws,
822	      )
823	      dq_entry.update({
824	        "bias_grad": False,
825	        "grad_kv_storage_dtype": None,
826	      })
827	      _record_entry(entries, dq_entry)
828	      tuned_entries.append((dq_entry, len(dq_wrapper.configs)))
829	
830	  return tuned_entries

833	def _build_payload(
834	  entries: list[dict[str, Any]],
835	  mode: str,
836	  batch: int,
837	  heads: int,
838	  full_tasks: bool,
839	  seqlens: list[int],
840	  enable_forward_tma: bool,
841	  enable_backward_tma: bool,
842	  enable_forward_ws: bool,
843	  enable_backward_ws: bool,
844	  enable_backward_split_launch: bool = False,
845	) -> dict[str, Any]:
846	  device_index = torch.cuda.current_device()
847	  device_name = torch.cuda.get_device_name(device_index)
848	  props = torch.cuda.get_device_properties(device_index)
849	  return {
850	    "schema_version": SCHEMA_VERSION,
851	    "device_name": device_name,
852	    "device_name_sanitized": sanitize_device_name(device_name),
853	    "compute_capability": f"{props.major}.{props.minor}",
854	    "generated_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
855	    "ffpa_version": __version__,
856	    "torch_version": torch.__version__,
857	    "triton_version": triton.__version__,
858	    "autotune_mode": mode,
859	    "B": batch,
860	    "H": heads,
861	    "full_tasks": full_tasks,
862	    "hardware_desc": {
863	      "enable_forward_tma": enable_forward_tma,
864	      "enable_backward_tma": enable_backward_tma,
865	      "enable_forward_ws": enable_forward_ws,
866	      "enable_backward_ws": enable_backward_ws,
867	      "enable_backward_split_launch": enable_backward_split_launch,
868	    },
869	    "tune_grid": {
870	      "headdims": DEFAULT_HEADDIMS,
871	      "seqlens": seqlens,
872	    },
873	    "entries": entries,
874	  }

877	def _parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
878	  parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
879	    description="Generate persistent FFPA Triton tuned configs."
880	  )
881	  parser.add_argument(
882	    "--mode",
883	    choices=("fast", "max"),
884	    default="fast",
885	    help="Triton autotune search-space mode.",
886	  )
887	  parser.add_argument(
888	    "--directions",
889	    choices=("forward", "backward", "both"),
890	    default="both",
891	  )
892	  parser.add_argument(
893	    "--B",
894	    type=int,
895	    default=1,
896	    help="Batch size used for tuning.",
897	  )
898	  parser.add_argument(
899	    "--H",
900	    type=int,
901	    default=32,
902	    help="Base query-head count used for tuning.",
903	  )
904	  parser.add_argument(
905	    "--full-tasks",
906	    action="store_true",
907	    help="Also tune canonical attn_mask, dropout, GQA, and MQA variants.",
908	  )
909	  parser.add_argument(
910	    "--dtypes",
911	    type=_parse_dtypes,
912	    default=[torch.bfloat16],
913	    help="Comma-separated dtypes: bf16,fp16.",
914	  )
915	  parser.add_argument(
916	    "--enable-tma",
917	    action="store_true",
918	    help="Compatibility alias for --enable-fwd-tma --enable-bwd-tma.",
919	  )
920	  parser.add_argument(
921	    "--enable-ws",
922	    action="store_true",
923	    help="Compatibility alias for --enable-fwd-ws --enable-bwd-ws.",
924	  )
925	  parser.add_argument(
926	    "--enable-fwd-tma",
927	    action="store_true",
928	    help=
929	    "Also generate persistent configs for the SM90+ TMA forward path when supported.",
930	  )
931	  parser.add_argument(
932	    "--enable-bwd-tma",
933	    action="store_true",
934	    help=
935	    "Also generate persistent configs for the SM90+ TMA backward path when supported.",
936	  )
937	  parser.add_argument(
938	    "--enable-fwd-ws",
939	    action="store_true",
940	    help=
941	    "Force warp-specialized SM90+ TMA forward configs when --enable-fwd-tma is set.",
942	  )
943	  parser.add_argument(
944	    "--enable-bwd-ws",
945	    action="store_true",
946	    help=
947	    "Force warp-specialized SM90+ TMA backward configs when --enable-bwd-tma is set.",
948	  )
949	  parser.add_argument(
950	    "--enable-bwd-split-launch",
951	    "--bwd-split-launch",
952	    "--bwd-split",
953	    action="store_true",
954	    help=
955	    "Additionally tune SM90+ TMA backward split-launch dK/dV and dQ kernels when --enable-bwd-tma is set.",
956	  )
957	  parser.add_argument(
958	    "--overwrite",
959	    action="store_true",
960	    help="Overwrite an existing device config JSON.",
961	  )
962	  parser.add_argument(
963	    "--output-dir",
964	    type=Path,
965	    default=None,
966	    help="Directory for generated device JSON.",
967	  )
968	  parser.add_argument(
969	    "--num-gpus",
970	    type=int,
971	    default=None,
972	    help="Number of GPUs for parallel autotune via Ray. "
973	    "In local mode, must not exceed CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES count. "
974	    "In remote mode (--ray-address), requests GPUs from the cluster. "
975	    "Omit for single-GPU mode (no Ray dependency).",
976	  )
977	  parser.add_argument(
978	    "--ray-address",
979	    type=str,
980	    default=None,
981	    help="Optional Ray cluster address. Uses local Ray when unset.",
982	  )
983	  return _resolve_directional_cli_flags(parser.parse_args())

986	def main() -> int:
987	  """Run the autotune config generation CLI.
988	
989	  :return: Process exit code.
990	  """
991	  args = _parse_args()
992	  logger.info("Starting FFPA Triton autotune with args: %s", args)
993	  if args.B <= 0 or args.H <= 0:
994	    raise SystemExit("ffpa_attn.autotune requires positive --B and --H values")
995	  if not torch.cuda.is_available():
996	    raise SystemExit("ffpa_attn.autotune requires a CUDA device")
997	
998	  output_dir = args.output_dir or default_config_dir()
999	  output_path = device_config_path(output_dir)
1000	  if output_path.exists() and not args.overwrite:
1001	    logger.info("Tuned config already exists, not overwriting: %s", output_path)
1002	    return 0
1003	
1004	  seqlens = _available_seqlens()
1005	  tasks: list[TuneTask] = []
1006	  if args.directions in ("forward", "both"):
1007	    tasks.extend(
1008	      _iter_forward_tasks(
1009	        args.dtypes,
1010	        seqlens,
1011	        heads=args.H,
1012	        full_tasks=args.full_tasks,
1013	      )
1014	    )
1015	  if args.directions in ("backward", "both"):
1016	    tasks.extend(
1017	      _iter_backward_tasks(
1018	        args.dtypes,
1019	        seqlens,
1020	        heads=args.H,
1021	        full_tasks=args.full_tasks,
1022	      )
1023	    )
1024	  tasks = _limit_tasks(tasks)
1025	
1026	  entries: dict[tuple[Any, ...], dict[str, Any]] = {}
1027	  full_variant_count = sum(
1028	    task.case_name in {"attn-mask", "dropout", "gqa", "mqa"} for task in tasks
1029	  )
1030	  logger.info(
1031	    "Generating %d FFPA Triton tuned config task(s) for %s "
1032	    "mode=%s directions=%s B=%d H=%d full_tasks=%s full_variants=%d",
1033	    len(tasks),
1034	    output_path,
1035	    args.mode,
1036	    args.directions,
1037	    args.B,
1038	    args.H,
1039	    args.full_tasks,
1040	    full_variant_count,
1041	  )
1042	
1043	  if args.num_gpus is not None and args.num_gpus > 1:
1044	    # Ray multi-GPU path
1045	    try:
1046	      import ray  # noqa: F401  early check for a friendly error message
1047	    except ImportError:
1048	      raise SystemExit(
1049	        "ray is required when --num-gpus is set. Install: pip install ray"
1050	      )
1051	
1052	    from .ray import run_ray_autotune
1053	
1054	    all_entries = run_ray_autotune(tasks, args)
1055	    for entry in all_entries:
1056	      _record_entry(entries, entry)
1057	  else:
1058	    # Single-GPU path
1059	    tune_start_time = time.perf_counter()
1060	    # Offline persistent tuning deliberately bypasses runtime seqlen bucketing
1061	    # so target grid points such as 2048 are tuned on their exact shapes
1062	    # instead of being intercepted by a coarser online autotune bucket.
1063	    with exact_autotune_seqlen_keys():
1064	      for index, task in enumerate(tasks, start=1):
1065	        try:
1066	          start_time = time.perf_counter()
1067	          if task.direction == "forward":
1068	            tuned_entries = _tune_forward(
1069	              task,
1070	              args.B,
1071	              args.mode,
1072	              entries,
1073	              enable_tma=args.enable_fwd_tma,
1074	              enable_ws=args.enable_fwd_ws,
1075	            )
1076	          else:
1077	            tuned_entries = _tune_backward(
1078	              task,
1079	              args.B,
1080	              args.mode,
1081	              entries,
1082	              enable_tma=args.enable_bwd_tma,
1083	              enable_ws=args.enable_bwd_ws,
1084	              enable_split_launch=args.enable_bwd_split_launch,
1085	            )
1086	          torch.cuda.synchronize()
1087	          elapsed = time.perf_counter() - start_time
1088	          total_elapsed = time.perf_counter() - tune_start_time
1089	          for tuned_entry, choices_count in tuned_entries:
1090	            logger.info(
1091	              "[AUTOTUNED][%d/%d] %s, t=%.3fs, T=%.3fs",
1092	              index,
1093	              len(tasks),
1094	              _format_entry(tuned_entry, choices_count, args.B),
1095	              elapsed,
1096	              total_elapsed,
1097	            )
1098	        except RuntimeError as exc:
1099	          if "out of memory" not in str(exc).lower():
1100	            raise
1101	          elapsed = time.perf_counter() - start_time
1102	          total_elapsed = time.perf_counter() - tune_start_time
1103	          logger.info(
1104	            "[AUTOTUNE-SKIPPED][%d/%d] OOM after t=%.3fs, T=%.3fs: %s",
1105	            index,
1106	            len(tasks),
1107	            elapsed,
1108	            total_elapsed,
1109	            exc,
1110	          )
1111	          torch.cuda.empty_cache()
1112	
1113	  ordered_entries = sorted(
1114	    entries.values(),
1115	    key=lambda item: (
1116	      item["direction"],
1117	      item["kernel"],
1118	      item["dtype"],
1119	      item.get("case_name", "common"),
1120	      item["headdim"],
1121	      item["seqlen_q"],
1122	      item["seqlen_k"],
1123	      item["causal"],
1124	      item.get("nheads_q", 0),
1125	      item.get("nheads_kv", 0),
1126	      item.get("has_attn_bias", False),
1127	      item.get("has_dropout", False),
1128	    )
1129	  )
1130	  payload = _build_payload(
1131	    ordered_entries,
1132	    args.mode,
1133	    args.B,
1134	    args.H,
1135	    args.full_tasks,
1136	    seqlens,
1137	    args.enable_fwd_tma,
1138	    args.enable_bwd_tma,
1139	    args.enable_fwd_ws,
1140	    args.enable_bwd_ws,
1141	    args.enable_bwd_split_launch,
1142	  )
1143	  write_config_file(payload, output_path, overwrite=args.overwrite)
1144	  logger.info(
1145	    "Wrote %d tuned config entries to %s", len(ordered_entries), output_path
1146	  )
1147	  return 0
